rct_screen_evidence: "Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel… Patients were stratified according to age…"
intervention: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) plus docetaxel (75 mg/m² every 3 weeks for six cycles)

1.1.quote: "Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy with ADT plus docetaxel..." (randomization_section)
1.1.justification: The statement confirms random assignment but does not specify the method used, allowing a reasonable inference of randomness.

1.2.quote: "Patients were randomly assigned to ADT alone or to combination therapy..." (randomization_section)
1.2.justification: No details are provided about how allocation was concealed (e.g., central randomization, sealed envelopes), so the information is insufficient.

1.3.quote: "Baseline characteristics... Age median 64 vs 63; ECOG 0‑69.8% vs 69.2%; High‑volume disease 66.2% vs 63.6%; etc." (baseline_characteristics)
1.3.justification: Baseline variables are closely balanced between groups, indicating no evidence of a problem with the randomization process.

2.1.quote: "We assigned men with metastatic, hormone-sensitive prostate cancer to receive either ADT plus docetaxel ... or ADT alone." (Methods)
2.1.justification: Participants were explicitly told which treatment they received; no blinding or placebo was used.

2.2.quote: "We assigned men ... to receive either ADT plus docetaxel ... or ADT alone." (Methods)
2.2.justification: Healthcare providers administering the interventions knew the allocation because the treatments are distinct and no blinding procedures are described.

3.1.quote: "All randomly assigned patients were followed and included in the primary analysis of their assigned group." (Patients section)
3.1.justification: The report states that every participant randomized was followed and analyzed, indicating no missing outcome data for progression‑free survival.

4.1.justification: The outcome is measured using standard, validated criteria for disease progression and death.

4.2.quote: "Patients were enrolled... assigned... to receive either ADT plus docetaxel or ADT alone." (blinding_section)
4.2.justification: Both groups were followed with the same schedule and methods for assessing progression.

4.3.quote: "The study was designed in 2005 by the Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG; now part of ECOG‑ACRIN) and was approved by the institutional review board at each participating institution." (blinding_section)
4.3.justification: Blinding of outcome assessors is not reported.

4.4.justification: Not applicable because assessor blinding status is unknown.

5.1.quote: "With each amendment, the sample size was adjusted... An intention-to-treat analysis plan was used... Cox proportional-hazard models, stratified according to the factors described above, were used to estimate hazard ratios..." (Statistical Analysis Plan)
5.1.justification: No detailed pre‑specified analysis plan dated before unblinding is provided, and timing of finalization is unclear.

5.2.quote: "The median time to biochemical, symptomatic, or radiographic progression was 20.2 months... hazard ratio, 0.61; 95% CI, 0.51 to 0.72" (Results Section)
5.2.justification: Only a single progression‑free survival definition is reported; no alternative scales, definitions, or time points are presented.

5.3.quote: "Cox proportional-hazard models, stratified according to the factors described above, were used to estimate hazard ratios for time-to-event end points." (Statistical Analysis Plan)
5.3.justification: The analysis described is limited to a stratified Cox model; no other eligible analytic approaches (e.g., unadjusted, different covariate sets) are reported.

2.3.quote: "All randomly assigned patients were followed and included in the primary analysis of their assigned group." (Results)
2.3.justification: No evidence that the trial context caused protocol deviations; the report only notes a few patients did not start therapy, without attributing this to trial context.

2.6.quote: "An intention-to-treat analysis was conducted that included all randomly assigned patients regardless of eligibility and treatment status." (Statistical Analysis)
2.6.justification: The study explicitly states that all randomized participants were analyzed in their assigned groups, meeting ITT criteria.

domain_rationales.D1: Row: Any / NI / N-PN-NI -> Some concerns (concealment unclear)
domain_rationales.D2: Part1=Low (Part1 Low: awareness present/unclear but no trial-context deviations); Part2=Low (2.6=Y/PY (appropriate ITT analysis) -> Part2=Low)

overall_rationale: Some concerns in 3 domains (D1, D4, D5). Skill guidance: 3 or more domains with Some concerns is very likely an overall High judgment if the concerns substantially lower confidence. Probable High; FLAG FOR HUMAN REVIEW/CONFIRMATION.
